Title: Fine-tuning phosphatidic acid production for optimal plant stress responses

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PA promotes ROS production for plant immunity
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: PA binds and stabilizes RBOHD, decreasing ubiquitination and suppressing vacuolar degradation; increases plasma membrane localization
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: reviewed evidence from PA-binding/stability studies, ROS assays, immune phenotyping
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Acts as a PA effector whose stabilization by PA enhances ROS production and plant immunity
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: immune elicitation by flg22/chitin; pathogen attack
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential companion target for strengthening PA-mediated immune signaling, though not the main engineering focus here
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PA directly binds RBOHD. 2. PA stabilizes RBOHD by reducing ubiquitination/vacuolar degradation and increasing plasma membrane localization. 3. Stabilized RBOHD promotes ROS burst and immunity.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes abiotic stress responses including drought and salinity tolerance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DGK5 binds ABA2 and PA suppresses ABA2 activity while promoting ABA2 nuclear sequestration
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Reduced ABA biosynthesis can compromise abiotic stress tolerance
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: reviewed evidence from protein interaction studies, activity assays, localization analysis, stress phenotyping
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Its activity is suppressed by DGK5-generated PA, decreasing ABA production and weakening abiotic stress responses
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: drought; high salinity; ABA-related abiotic stress
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important cautionary node when engineering DGK5 because altered PA can suppress ABA biosynthesis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ABA2 is a key enzyme in major ABA biosynthesis. 2. DGK5 binds ABA2 and the generated PA suppresses ABA2 activity. 3. PA also promotes ABA2 nuclear sequestration, lowering ABA accumulation and abiotic stress tolerance.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PA functions in stress signaling; controlled conversion to CDP-DAG can influence immunity-growth balance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Rice example cited to illustrate fine-tuning of PA-metabolizing enzymes by multiplex genome editing; optimal allele RBL1Δ12 breaks immunity–growth tradeoff
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: rbl1 mutant shows robust immunity but severe yield penalty; edited allele RBL1Δ12 retains resistance without yield loss
Common_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: rice mutant collection; specific cultivar NA
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: mutant identification and multiplex genome editing
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: PA-metabolizing enzyme converting PA to CDP-DAG
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Fine-tuned alteration of PA metabolism by RBL1 editing can improve disease resistance while avoiding yield penalty
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Proof-of-concept for editing PA metabolism genes to break stress/yield tradeoffs; conceptually supports DGK5 engineering
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RBL1 encodes a PA-metabolizing enzyme converting PA to CDP-DAG. 2. Strong loss-of-function improves immunity but reduces yield. 3. Multiplex editing generated RBL1Δ12 with broad-spectrum resistance and no yield penalty.

Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: quantitative lipidomics; single-cell lipidomics; subcellular lipidomics; single-cell transcriptomics; real-time PA biosensor imaging (PAleon)
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: DGK5-mediated PA production enhances biotic stress resistance but suppresses abiotic stress tolerance; dgk5 knockout improves tolerance to drought/high salinity/freezing-related abiotic stress contexts while potentially compromising immune PA signaling
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Produces phosphatidic acid that promotes immune ROS signaling and pathogen resistance, but negatively regulates abiotic stress tolerance by suppressing ABA biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Key engineering target for fine-tuning PA production; coding-sequence editing, phosphomimic allele design, promoter editing, PrimeRoot insertion, CRISPRi/Cas13a downregulation proposed to optimize tradeoff between biotic and abiotic stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: DGK5β transcript induced under chitin treatment; stress-responsive context emphasized
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: reviewed evidence from knockout mutants, phosphosite mutagenesis/phosphomimic variants, protein-binding assays, PA quantification, PA biosensor imaging, ROS assays, pathogen immunity assays, ABA/abiotic stress phenotyping
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: phosphorylates diacylglycerol to generate phosphatidic acid
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: diacylglycerol kinase; calmodulin-binding domain present in DGK5β isoform
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: DGK5β transcript responsive to chitin; DGK5 involved in biotic and abiotic stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phosphatidic acid acts as a membrane lipid and second messenger in plant stress signaling, including ROS-mediated immunity and abiotic stress responses
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DGK5 activity is oppositely tuned by phosphorylation at Ser506 (activating, by BIK1/RIPK) and Thr446 (inhibitory, by MPK4); DGK5β rather than DGK5α mediates chitin-induced PA production due to calmodulin-binding domain
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: biotic elicitation by flg22, chitin, bacterial and fungal pathogens; abiotic stress including drought, high salinity, freezing; ABA-related conditions
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. DGK5 converts DAG to PA and is a central source of stress-responsive PA in Arabidopsis. 2. BIK1/RIPK-mediated phosphorylation activates DGK5 whereas MPK4 phosphorylation suppresses it, fine-tuning PA homeostasis during immunity. 3. DGK5-derived PA stabilizes RBOHD and promotes ROS production and pathogen resistance. 4. DGK5-derived PA suppresses ABA2 function and ABA production, contributing negatively to abiotic stress tolerance.

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: reviewed evidence from phosphorylation analysis, phosphosite mutagenesis, immune elicitation assays, PA/ROS measurements
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: phosphorylates DGK5 at Ser506 to activate its PA-producing activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes DGK5-mediated phosphatidic acid production and downstream ROS-dependent immunity
Regulation_Genes.Protein_Complex_Involvement: functions downstream of immune receptor complexes such as FLS2 signaling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PA supports stress signaling and promotes immune ROS burst
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Enhanced immune signaling may antagonize abiotic stress optimization through increased DGK5-PA output
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: condition-dependent activator of DGK5-driven PA production during immunity
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BIK1 is activated in immune signaling downstream of elicitor perception. 2. BIK1 phosphorylates DGK5 at Ser506. 3. This phosphorylation enhances DGK5 activity, increasing PA and immune ROS signaling.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: DGK5 phosphomimic engineering can mimic BIK1 activation to enhance disease resistance
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Discussed as upstream regulator of DGK5 rather than primary engineering target in this review
Regulation_Genes.Upstream_Signals_or_Inputs: flg22 perception by FLS2; pathogen attack

Regulation_Genes.Feedback_or_Feedforward_Regulation: homeostatic negative regulation of PA production
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: reviewed evidence from phosphorylation analysis, phosphosite mutagenesis, immune signaling assays
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: phosphorylates DGK5 at Thr446 to suppress its activity
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Restrains DGK5-mediated phosphatidic acid production during immune responses
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PA acts as stress-signaling lipid; controlled accumulation prevents imbalance
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Suppression of PA may limit immune ROS output but help avoid excess PA-associated costs
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: negative controller that fine-tunes DGK5-dependent PA homeostasis
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. MPK4 is induced in immune signaling. 2. MPK4 phosphorylates DGK5 at Thr446. 3. This modification suppresses DGK5 activity and limits PA production.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Editing DGK5 Thr446 site to prevent MPK4 suppression is proposed to boost immunity
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts antagonistically to BIK1 on DGK5

Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: review summary citing phosphorylation-based activation of DGK5 in biotic stress context
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes DGK5-mediated PA production during biotic stress signaling
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: PA promotes immune signaling and ROS production
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: supporting activator of DGK5-dependent PA accumulation in immune response
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. RIPK is presented as an upstream kinase in biotic stress signaling. 2. RIPK phosphorylates/activates DGK5 at Ser506 in the model figure. 3. This supports PA accumulation for immunity.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited direct value in this review; mainly informs DGK5 phosphosite engineering
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mentioned in figure/model rather than deeply discussed in text
